/uses

The tools I use every day

A living inventory of the hardware, editor setup, and services I rely on to design, build, and ship software. Updated as things change — last edited April 2026.

Currently happy with this setup
Hardware Editor & Terminal Development Design & Productivity Services & Hosting
01 — Category

Hardware

The machines and peripherals on my desk.

  • MacBook Pro 14" (M3 Pro) 14-inch, 36GB RAM, 1TB SSD — my daily driver for all things code.
  • LG 27UP850 4K Monitor 27-inch 4K IPS for a sharp, true-to-color workspace.
  • Keychron K3 Pro Low-profile mechanical, brown switches, wireless.
  • Logitech MX Master 3S Silent clicks, flawless scroll, perfect for long sessions.
  • Sony WH-1000XM5 Noise-cancelling headphones for deep work.
  • iPhone 15 Pro Mobile testing and on-the-go life.
02 — Category

Editor & Terminal

Where the real work happens.

  • Visual Studio Code Primary editor. Theme: One Dark Pro. Font: JetBrains Mono.
  • Neovim For quick edits and server-side work — LazyVim config.
  • Warp Modern terminal with blocks, AI, and team features.
  • zsh + starship Fast, minimal prompt with git + language context baked in.
  • JetBrains Mono Ligature-friendly, readable at every size.
03 — Category

Development

Tools that ship code to production.

  • Git + GitHub Source control and collaboration for every project.
  • Docker + Compose Local dev environments and production containers.
  • pnpm Fast, disk-efficient package manager. Default for new projects.
  • PostgreSQL Go-to relational database for most backend work.
  • Postman API exploration, testing, and collection sharing.
  • 1Password Passwords, SSH keys, and dev secrets — all in one place.
04 — Category

Design & Productivity

For thinking, planning, and shipping polish.

  • Figma Design, prototype, and handoff. Where every UI starts.
  • Raycast Launcher, clipboard history, and scripts — faster than Spotlight.
  • Obsidian Second brain — daily notes, project docs, and long-form writing.
  • Notion Team docs, project tracking, and shared knowledge bases.
  • Linear Issue tracking that actually feels fast.
05 — Category

Services & Hosting

Where things run in production.

  • Vercel Deploy frontends and edge functions in seconds.
  • Cloudflare DNS, CDN, Workers, and R2 for global performance.
  • Railway Simple, scalable hosting for backends and databases.
  • Supabase Postgres + auth + storage — my default BaaS for side projects.
  • Spotify Lo-fi and focus playlists for the daily grind.

Have a tool I should try?

I'm always curious about new gear and workflow improvements. If you have a recommendation, I'd love to hear it.

Send me a tip

Full Stack Developer building scalable web applications with modern technologies.
